meta.title: Font Construction — Emergence → Form → System → Glyph Architecture → Letter Design → Usage → Linguistic Cases
meta.subtitle: A lattice of constraints showing how a complete typeface emerges from abstract possibility through formal systems to concrete linguistic expression.
meta.searchPlaceholder: glyph, stroke contrast, serif, sans-serif, x-height, ascender, descender, aperture, counter, kerning, ligature, diacritic, orthography, phoneme, grapheme
meta.userContextPlaceholder: e.g., text face for academic publishing; display font; variable font; Latin + IPA support
meta.promptTemplate:
You are an expert type designer and typographic systems theorist.

Explain how high-level constraints shape a complete font from abstraction to linguistic use.
Use the selected concepts to show how upstream design decisions constrain downstream glyphs and usage scenarios.

levels.title: Ontological emergence
levels.hint: From nothing to the idea that marks can signify.

levels.title: Foundational abstractions
levels.hint: Meaning, difference, rhythm, and visual contrast.

levels.title: Typographic systems
levels.hint: Rules that make many glyphs belong to one font.

levels.title: Specific letterforms
levels.hint: Design of individual glyphs and variants.

nodes.label: The possibility of a mark
nodes.summary: That something can appear against nothing and be perceived as distinct.
nodes.bodyHtml: All fonts begin with the possibility that a visible difference can exist and be noticed.

nodes.label: Difference and recognition
nodes.summary: Marks must be distinguishable to carry meaning.

nodes.label: Systemic consistency
nodes.summary: A font is a coherent system, not isolated drawings.
nodes.bodyHtml: Shared proportions, rhythm and logic bind glyphs into one font.

nodes.label: Font metrics
nodes.summary: Baseline, x-height, ascenders, descenders and alignment zones.
nodes.bodyHtml: These invisible lines constrain all letter construction.

nodes.label: Lowercase ‘a’
nodes.summary: A specific glyph constrained by system metrics and stylistic decisions.
nodes.bodyHtml: The form of ‘a’ expresses the font’s character while obeying system rules.

nodes.label: Spacing & kerning behaviour
nodes.summary: How glyphs interact horizontally in text.
nodes.bodyHtml: Quality emerges from consistent spacing under varied conditions.

nodes.label: Academic English paragraph
nodes.summary: The font used in running scholarly text.
nodes.bodyHtml: This is the final test: sustained reading in a real language.

edges.why: A mark must be different to be perceived.
Systems become concrete through shared metrics.
Individual glyph shapes affect spacing quality.
Spacing quality determines readability in real language use.
